name: Cloud Gate (The Bean)
description: The most photographed object in Chicago -- Anish Kapoor 33-foot polished steel sculpture in Millennium Park that reflects the skyline like liquid mercury. Free, open daily, and somehow never gets old. Go early morning for the best photos without crowds. The underside creates a trippy funhouse mirror effect.

name: Willis Tower Skydeck
description: 103rd floor of the former Sears Tower -- the highest public viewing area in the United States. The Ledge is a glass box extending 4.3 feet out from the building, 1,353 feet above the ground. On clear days you can see four states. Not for the faint of heart.

name: Art Institute of Chicago
description: One of the oldest and largest art museums in the world with 300,000+ works spanning 5,000 years. Seurat Sunday Afternoon, Hopper Nighthawks, and Grant Wood American Gothic are all here. The Modern Wing by Renzo Piano is architectural perfection. Allow at least 3 hours.
dishes: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte

name: Navy Pier
description: 3,300 feet of lakefront entertainment stretching into Lake Michigan with a 200-foot Ferris wheel, restaurants, museums, and boat tours. Touristy yes, but the Centennial Wheel ride at sunset with the skyline behind you is genuinely magical. Free to walk the pier.

name: Magnificent Mile
description: 13 blocks of Michigan Avenue from the Chicago River to Oak Street -- luxury shopping, landmark architecture, and people-watching. The Water Tower (one of the few buildings to survive the Great Fire) and Tribune Tower are highlights. Holiday lights in December are spectacular.

name: Chicago Architecture River Cruise
description: The single best thing a tourist can do in Chicago -- a 90-minute boat tour down the Chicago River with expert guides explaining 130+ years of architectural history. See Marina City, the Wrigley Building, Tribune Tower, and dozens more. Book the Chicago Architecture Foundation tour for the best guides.

name: Millennium Park
description: 24.5 acres of world-class public art, architecture, and gardens in downtown Chicago. Beyond The Bean, there is Jay Pritzker Pavilion (Frank Gehry concert venue), Crown Fountain (interactive video towers), and Lurie Garden. Free concerts all summer. 25 million visitors per year.

name: Lincoln Park Zoo
description: One of the last free major zoos in the United States, sitting on 35 acres in the heart of Lincoln Park. Gorillas, lions, penguins, and a farm -- all free. The zoo has been open since 1868 making it one of the oldest in North America. ZooLights in winter is magical.

name: Wrigley Field
description: The Friendly Confines -- home of the Cubs since 1914 and the second-oldest MLB ballpark in America. The ivy-covered outfield walls, the manual scoreboard, and the rooftop seats across Waveland Avenue are uniquely Chicago. A game here is a pilgrimage, not just a sporting event.

name: Chicago Riverwalk
description: 1.25 miles of waterfront path along the Chicago River with wine bars, kayak rentals, restaurants, and public art. City Winery has a riverfront tasting room. Rent a kayak to see the city from water level. The best free walk in Chicago, especially at sunset.
